title: m3 meta_cert_anchor_log row schema
description: JSON schema for one append-only row in state/meta_cert_anchor_log.jsonl. Each row records one external trust anchor publication event (git tag + detached signature) per spec/m3_external_anchor_workflow.md. Append-only; fsync per row; gitignored under state/. Truncates Tarski meta-verifier regress at V_1 by epistemic decree (ordinal stamp ω·2+2).

ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp (with offset) of the anchor publication event, recorded by the operator at tag-push time. NOT trusted as the canonical anchor time — the canonical timestamp is GitHub's server-side receipt, not this field.

chain_head_hash.description: sha256 (lowercase hex, 64 chars) of the last row of state/meta_decision_cert.jsonl at anchor time. Equals the annotation message of the corresponding git tag exactly (no prefix, no trailing newline).

tag_name.description: Git tag name, format meta-cert-anchor-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<short_hash> where short_hash = chain_head_hash[:12]. UTC.

signed_by_keyid.description: Fingerprint or long-form keyid of the PGP/SSH key used by `git tag -s`. Allows consumers to map signatures back to a known operator identity and to detect post-compromise rotations (§5 of workflow spec).

from_round.description: Inclusive lower bound: first cert round (R-number) covered by this anchor. Equals previous anchor's to_round + 1, or 0 for the first anchor ever.

to_round.description: Inclusive upper bound: last cert round covered by this anchor — i.e., the round whose row's content-address is chain_head_hash.

anchored_round_range.description: Round span this anchor commits to. Verifier must re-hash every cert row in [from_round, to_round] and confirm the chain reproduces chain_head_hash exactly. Any divergence falsifies the anchor (see m3_anchor_falsifier.md).

verification_command.description: Literal shell command consumers run to re-verify this anchor independently. Conventionally: `git fetch --tags && git tag -v <tag_name>`. Stored verbatim per row so future schema additions don't break old verifiers' replay paths.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this row (e.g., 'v1', 'v1.1'). Append-only evolution: new fields → new schema_version; old rows unchanged. raw 77 audit-append-only invariant.

notes.description: Optional free-form annotation. Used for key-rotation events (referencing previous tag_name under old keyid), known clock-skew, or operator commentary. Not load-bearing for verification.
